title: MCS Sky Palace 4

ingest_warnings: 1.17+ downsample applied to 8 block ids: minecraft:calcite->minecraft:diorite, minecraft:decorated_pot->minecraft:flower_pot, minecraft:jigsaw->minecraft:stone, minecraft:mangrove_fence->minecraft:dark_oak_fence, minecraft:mangrove_log->minecraft:dark_oak_log, minecraft:mangrove_stairs->minecraft:dark_oak_stairs, minecraft:mangrove_trapdoor->minecraft:dark_oak_trapdoor, minecraft:moss_carpet->minecraft:green_carpet

description:
This small, two-storey structure, measuring 22x46 cells and 23 cells high, resembles a miniature Chinese-style pagoda or sky palace, floating on an island of earth and stone. The main body of the building is a square block with walls of smooth quartz and birch planks, giving it a light, airy appearance. A prominent red terracotta tile roof with wide eaves and slightly upturned corners extends around the upper storey, with small red concrete accents on the corners. Above this, a smaller, recessed square structure with stone brick and diorite trim rises, forming an open-air upper level. The entire building rests on a base of grass blocks and jungle leaves, creating a small garden effect, which in turn sits atop a rugged foundation of stone and coarse dirt, suggesting a floating island.

The interior of this sky palace is implied to be simple, with a main floor and an accessible upper level. The ground floor appears to be a single, open room, likely serving as a common area, with large window-like openings on all sides, suggesting ample natural light. A smaller, open room is present on the upper level, surrounded by a low wall, providing an observation deck or a more private space. The overall design, with its light-colored walls, open structure, and elevated position, suggests a serene and contemplative atmosphere, characteristic of a fantasy-inspired Chinese architectural style.
